commit.message:
feat: LifeAxe Phase 1 — AI-powered guide creation platform

Monorepo with pnpm workspaces:
- apps/admin: Vite SPA with TanStack Router, Supabase Auth, Tailwind CSS
- packages/database: Prisma schema + repository layer
- packages/providers: LLM (Claude) + Storage (Supabase) abstractions
- packages/services: Content generation + product management
- packages/ui: Component library + @react-pdf/renderer PDF templates

Features: auth, dashboard, product CRUD, three-panel editor,
AI content generation (Supabase Edge Function), branded PDF export.

* Restructure registry management as Claude skill

- Created .claude/skills/registry/SKILL.md with agent-focused documentation
- Moved scripts to .claude/skills/registry/ directory

- manage-registry.ts: list, get, delete, verify products
- configure-linear.ts: auto-configure Linear project names

This makes it clear the tools are for agent use, not manual user commands.

commit.message:
fix: prevent WhatsApp automation errors from failing lead capture

- Make mapBehaviorToLead and routeLeadByOwnership non-blocking in submitLead
  (WA automation failures are warnings, not lead capture failures)
- Fix persistOutboundMessage: use candidate payloads so template_name column
  absence in crm_whatsapp_messages doesn't throw and kill the request
- Fix executeWhatsApp in journey service: add fallback candidates without
  template_name (both previous candidates included it, so both failed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.1.2: Start reconnect watchdog at serve() startup

Previously _schedule_reconnect_check() was only called from _on_datagram(),
so if the wallbox was already silent at HA startup (e.g. after an HA restart),
the watchdog never started and no wakeup broadcast was ever sent.

Now serve() initialises last_message_time and schedules the first check
immediately after binding the UDP socket. After message_timeout seconds
without a datagram the wakeup broadcast is sent automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[Spark] Add VariantType support in schema conversion (#6164)

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!  Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, please read our contributor guidelines:
https://github.com/delta-io/delta/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md
2. If the PR is unfinished, add '[WIP]' in your PR title, e.g., '[WIP]
Your PR title ...'.
  3. Be sure to keep the PR description updated to reflect all changes.
  4. Please write your PR title to summarize what this PR proposes.
5. If possible, provide a concise example to reproduce the issue for a
faster review.
6. If applicable, include the corresponding issue number in the PR title
and link it in the body.
-->

#### Which Delta project/connector is this regarding?
<!--
Please add the component selected below to the beginning of the pull
request title
For example: [Spark] Title of my pull request
-->

- [x] Spark
- [ ] Standalone
- [ ] Flink
- [ ] Kernel
- [ ] Other (fill in here)

## Description

<!--
- Describe what this PR changes.
- Describe why we need the change.
 
If this PR resolves an issue be sure to include "Resolves #XXX" to
correctly link and close the issue upon merge.
-->

Map kernel's VariantType to/from Spark's VariantType (available since
Spark 4.0) in SchemaUtils. The V2 connector uses Spark's own parquet
reader for data reading, so no kernel-level parquet changes are needed —
Spark already handles reading variant columns natively.

## How was this patch tested?

<!--
If tests were added, say they were added here. Please make sure to test
the changes thoroughly including negative and positive cases if
possible.
If the changes were tested in any way other than unit tests, please
clarify how you tested step by step (ideally copy and paste-able, so
that other reviewers can test and check, and descendants can verify in
the future).
If the changes were not tested, please explain why.
-->

Unit test

## Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing changes?

<!--
If yes, please clarify the previous behavior and the change this PR
proposes - provide the console output, description and/or an example to
show the behavior difference if possible.
If possible, please also clarify if this is a user-facing change
compared to the released Delta Lake versions or within the unreleased
branches such as master.
If no, write 'No'.
-->

No

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add Sourcegraph query dialect comparison and porting guide

Document differences between CodeDB's query language and Sourcegraph's,
including unsupported repository-level filters (fork:, archived:, etc.),
regex, boolean operators, and structural search. Add a porting table
mapping common Sourcegraph queries to CodeDB equivalents.

commit.message:
Implement output_parser: full structured block extraction

Replace the one-line stub with a real parser that scans agent responses
for fenced blocks (VAULT_UPDATE, PROPOSAL, REVIEW, SUMMARY) and extracts
typed structs. Handles simple key:value, [list], and key:| multi-line
fields with 2-space indent stripping. Everything outside blocks goes into
free_text. Lenient on unclosed blocks.

Add tests/unit/test_output_parser.cpp (6 test functions, 33 assertions,
all passing via ctest). Wire target into CMakeLists.txt.

Update DEVELOPMENT.md and CLAUDE.md to reflect that invoker, task queue,
context assembler, and output parser are all now complete (items 3-6).

Add /commit and /commit-push workflow skills

Two new skills for committing changes across both the agent repo
and skills subrepo. /commit stages and commits without pushing,
/commit-push does both. Both include pre-commit checks (secrets,
frontmatter validation, large files, symlink health) and handle
remote divergence with conflict resolution prompts.

commit.message:
iOS: pre-split text at clause boundaries like Android/NVDA

Replace eSpeak's opaque clause chunking with explicit pre-splitting
at punctuation boundaries. Colon/semicolon only split when followed
by whitespace (preserves times like "5:44"). Each clause gets its
own eSpeak call with combined IPA accumulation for proper text
parser alignment. Fixes issue #40 where "Unread: 1" could leak the
trailing number into VoiceOver's next utterance.

Fix word lists, add state persistence, improve drag handling and accessibility

- Word lists: remove duplicates, spaces, over-length and obscure words;
  fix spelling (CRAPE→CREPE), use British English (TRUCK→MOPED)
- State persistence: save/restore puzzles, progress and category order
  via localStorage so refreshing the page doesn't lose progress
- Drag handling: add setPointerCapture and document-level pointerup
  safety net to prevent stuck selection state
- Bug fixes: off-by-one in showComplete, remove recursive fallback
  in generatePuzzle, add setCurrent helper for atomic index updates
- Accessibility: ARIA dialog roles, grid label, focus-visible outlines
- Service worker: only cache successful (response.ok) fetches
- Tests: add persistence and completion edge-case tests
- Bump to v5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix modal scroll on mobile/touch + add names, images, SW/MAN to all products

- Remove position:fixed body-lock (was breaking touch scroll on iOS Safari)
  Replace with overflow:hidden on html+body — background stays locked, modal scrolls
- Add touch-action:pan-y + overscroll-behavior:contain to .modal-body
- Remove competing overflow-y:auto from .modal-info (single scroll container)
- 170 products: all named, CDN images fixed (K550W, K550-Purple, K565R, K555, K568-B,
  K579, K592), SW/MAN added (K552 family, K565R, K589, K592, K617, K628, K619, K718,

commit.message:
chore(ci): add workflow to update lockfile on Dependabot PRs

Dependabot updates individual package.json files but does not regenerate
the workspace-level pnpm-lock.yaml, causing CI to fail with frozen
lockfile mismatch. This workflow runs pnpm install and commits the
updated lockfile back to the Dependabot branch.
